action.reasoning: Observation: Eval failed because predict.py uses default base path '/data/base_model/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct' but the eval container only mounts test data at /data; the base model isn't necessarily there. Wait — the task says base model is at /data/base_model/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct. But the error says 'Repo id must be in the form...' meaning it tried to treat path as repo. That means the path didn't exist. Let me check: in eval, data_path is /data with test_prompts.jsonl. Base model must come from checkpoint dir or be bundled. Analysis: I need to bundle base model with checkpoint OR detect base path. Best: copy/use the base model that ships with checkpoint. Actually the eval container should also have /data/base_model. Let me check error more carefully - 'OSError: Repo id must be in the form...' — the path '/data/base_model/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct' doesn't exist in eval container. The eval container's /data only has test data, not base model. Decision: I need to make checkpoint self-contained. Save merged model into checkpoint dir, OR copy base model files into lora_ckpt. Simpler: merge LoRA into base and save full model to a checkpoint dir. But that's 6GB. Actually simplest: save base model files alongside lora adapter in checkpoint dir, then predict.py loads base from checkpoint dir.
